what: A shiny Application for Network Meta-analysis
title: A shiny Application for Network Meta-analysis
text: Network meta-analysis (NMA) increasingly adopted evidence-based medicine compare multiple interventions address questions ‘intervention ‘best’ overall?’. Currently, NMA primarily conducted statistical packages WinBUGs, R STATA, software coding can difficult non- statisticians hinders progress carrying systematic reviews containing NMA. MetaInsight tool conducts NMA via web requiring specialist software user install leveraging established analysis routines (specifically netmeta gemtc package R). tool interactive uses intuitive ‘point click’ interface presents results visually intuitive appealing ways. hoped tool assist conducting NMA expert statistical programming skills, , turn, increase relevance published meta-analyses, long term contribute improved healthcare decision making result. use app please cite : Owen, RK, Bradbury, N, Xin, Y, Cooper, N, Sutton, . MetaInsight: interactive web-based tool analyzing, interrogating, visualizing network meta-analyses using R-shiny netmeta. Res Syn Meth. 2019; 10: 569-581. Please consult Wiki information using MetaInsight.

title: Fit a baseline risk regression model — baseline_model
text: configured_data list. Input dataset created setup_configure() setup_exclude() regressor_type character. Type regression coefficient, either shared, unrelated, exchangeable n_iter numeric. Number simulation iterations. Defaults 20000 can normally left unchanged max_iter numeric. maximum number iterations. Defaults 60000 can normally left unchanged. check_iter numeric. number iterations convergence checked . Defaults 10000 can normally left unchanged. async Whether function used asynchronously. Default FALSE

title: Fit a baseline risk regression model — baseline_model
text: List bnma related output: mtcResults model object carried (needed match existing code) covariate_value mean covariate value, used centring reference_treatment character. reference_treatmentfrom configured_data comparator_names Vector containing names comparators text output stating whether fixed random effects cov_value_sentence text output stating value covariate set producing output slopes named list slopes regression equations (unstandardised - equal one 'increment') intercepts named list intercepts regression equations cov_value outcome character. outcome configured_data outcome_measure character. outcome_measurefrom configured_data effects character. effects configured_data covariate_min Vector minimum covariate values directly contributing regression covariate_max Vector maximum covariate values directly contributing regression dic Summary model fit sumresults Output summary(model) regressor Type regression coefficient

code: freq_inconsistency(configured_data, logger = NULL)

title: Inconsistency tables for frequentist models — freq_inconsistency
text: configured_data list. Input dataset created setup_configure() setup_exclude() logger Stores notification messages displayed Log Window. Insert logger reactive list running shiny, otherwise leave default NULL

title: Inconsistency tables for frequentist models — freq_inconsistency
text: Dataframe inconsistency data: Comparison Treatment comparison .Studies Number studies NMA NMA treatment effect estimate Direct Direct treatment effect estimate Indirect Indirect treatment effect estimate Difference Difference treatment effects Diff_95CI_lower 2.5% limit difference treatment effects Diff_95CI_upper 97.5% limit difference treatment effects pValue p-value test "difference treatment effects == 0"

title: Load data — setup_load
text: Load data spreadsheet default dataset assess data validity. checks column names required columns balanced wide format numbered columns. Data can either long wide format; long data one row per study arm whereas wide data one row per study. continuous outcomes, long data contain columns: Study - identifier, e.g. author year, T - treatment, N - number participants, Mean - mean value outcome, SD - standard deviation outcome. Wide data continuous outcomes contain: Study, N.1, N.2, Mean.1, Mean.2, SD.1, SD.2 number refers arm study extra columns added depending number arms. binary outcomes, long data contain: Study, T, N (continuous data) R - number participants outcome interest. Wide data binary outcomes follow convention: Study, T.1, T.2, R.1, R.2, N.1, N.2. Additionally, covar.<name> column can added formats containing covariate data  replaced name covariate. long data, covariate values must equal every study arm. Risk bias data can also included columns containing values ranging 1 (low risk) 3 (high risk): rob overall risk bias, indirectness indirectness rob.<name> ten individual components.

text: configured_data list. Input dataset created setup_configure() setup_exclude() plot_area_width numeric. width plot area containing treatment effects inches. Defaults 6. colourblind logical. Whether use colourblind-friendly palette. Defaults FALSE x_min numeric. Minimum value x-axis. Defaults NULL. binary outcomes values wrapped log() x_max numeric. Maximum value x-axis. Defaults NULL. binary outcomes values wrapped log() interactive logical. Whether plot altered preparation interactive interface. Defaults FALSE logger Stores notification messages displayed Log Window. Insert logger reactive list running shiny, otherwise leave default NULL
